commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=01c03e0707274c6a0914b6444f93aa99a622db8a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes Telegram forwarded-burst debounce resolution to use the inbound debounce setting with an 80 ms floor and updates the forwarded debounce cancellation regression test.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +2. Total +4 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the changed resolver still floors every forwarded entry with `Math.max(debounceMs, 80)`, while the config schema accepts positive debounce windows below 80 ms.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed for real Telegram proof, and the small timing correction should be reviewed before this draft can become land-ready.

target.title: fix(telegram): honor inbound debounce for forwarded bursts
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Telegram already supports native inbound debouncing through `messages.inbound`, but forwarded/shared-message bursts used a separate Telegram adapter lane with a hardcoded 80ms delay. When Telegram delivered split chunks more than 80ms apart, OpenClaw could dispatch them as separate turns even when the channel configured a longer native quiet window.

This change makes the forwarded/shared lane honour the configured native Telegram inbound debounce window and keeps 80ms only as the fallback when native inbound debouncing is disabled.

## Summary

- make Telegram forwarded/shared-message burst dispatch honour the configured native inbound debounce window
- keep the previous 80ms delay only as the fallback when native inbound batching is disabled
- add regression coverage for forwarded burst coalescing

## Evidence

- `npx oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts`
- Targeted Vitest file passed 111 tests locally on the clean PR branch.
target.author: moeedahmed
